questions.question: The term “middle-technology trap” was making news in context with which country recently?
questions.explanation: A top Chinese think tank warned of a potential “middle-technology trap” where China’s manufacturing sector gets hamstrung at low and mid-end technologies, struggling to catch up on core innovations retained by developed nations. This highlights worries that China may face economic stagnation challenges in transitioning to a high-income economy, as technological constraints persist despite industrial transfers reaping benefits from low-cost advantages so far.

questions.question: The Lingaraj Temple, that was recently making news, is located in which city of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Odisha government launched the Lingaraj Ekamra Kshetra Amenities and Monuments Revival Action (EKAMRA) scheme in January 2020, focused on restoring and improving amenities at the 11th century Lingaraj Temple and 30 other shrines across 80 acres in Bhubaneswar. It aims to enhance the experience for devotees while preserving heritage value, including beautification, better access roads and removing encroachments. The Lingaraj temple holds religious significance as a prominent Hindu and tourist site. This scheme elevates Bhubaneswar’s status as a “Temple City” while boosting Odisha’s cultural identity under CM Naveen Patnaik’s initiative to rejuvenate spiritual centers across the state.

questions.question: Salton Sea, that was recently making news for huge Lithium deposits, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Lithium, which is crucial for electric vehicle batteries, is called “white gold” and has been discovered in rich deposits worth an estimated $540 billion deep underground California’s Salton Sea in United States. This could position the US as a global lithium leader with reserves to supply millions of EVs. However, careful extraction is needed to prevent environmental impact on the inland lake and address community concerns. If harnessed responsibly, these lithium reserves could revolutionize clean energy storage and propel the transition to electric mobility.

questions.question: Which ministry has recently initiated the ‘Dare to Dream’ scheme in honor of the late former President Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam?
questions.explanation: The Dare to Dream (D2D) scheme is a contest organized by the Ministry of Defence in India, specifically by the Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO). The contest aims to encourage innovation and creativity among individual innovators and start-ups. The Dare to Dream Contest, initiated in memory of former President Dr APJ Abdul Kalam. Launched in 2019, the contest provides a unique opportunity for start-ups, innovators, entrepreneurs, and individuals above 18 years old to address key challenges in emerging technologies, ultimately contributing to enhancing India’s defence and aerospace capabilities. The evaluation criteria for entries include completeness of proposal, scientific soundness, design completeness, merit, technological readiness level achieved, and innovation. The contest aims to bring together a diverse group of participants to foster innovation and creativity in addressing technological challenges relevant to defence and aerospace.

questions.question: India conducts military exercises Al Najah, Eastern Bridge and Naseem Al Bahr with which Arab country?
questions.explanation: Recently, India and Oman have adopted the India Oman Joint Vision Partnership For the Future, setting the stage for bilateral cooperation and charting pathways for future collaboration between the two countries. This Vision Document focuses on building partnerships in broadly 8 to 10 areas. These include maritime cooperation and connectivity, energy security, space, digital payments, health, tourism, hospitality, agriculture and food security. The Joint Military Cooperation Committee (JMCC) is the highest forum of engagement between India and Oman in the field of defence. Military Exercises: Army exercise: Al Najah Air Force exercise: Eastern Bridge Naval Exercise: Naseem Al Bahr

questions.question: What is the objective of the recently established “Operation Prosperity Guardian” initiative?

options.label: A
options.text: Strengthen economic ties between neighboring countries in Europe

options.label: B
options.text: Combat illegal fishing and environmental hazards in the Indian Ocean

options.label: C
options.text: Curb maritime security problems by Houthi rebels in the Red Sea region

options.label: D
options.text: Promote commercial shipping and trade routes in the Mediterranean Sea

answer.label: C
answer.text: Curb maritime security problems by Houthi rebels in the Red Sea region

questions.explanation: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin announced the establishment of Operation Prosperity Guardian, a new US-led multinational security initiative under the Combined Maritime Forces, focusing on maritime security challenges in the Red Sea region. This comes following recent attacks on commercial shipping by Iran-aligned Houthi rebels based in Yemen using drones and missiles. The operation aims to ensure freedom of navigation in response to the escalating security crisis. Before that, the Swan Atlantic, a Cayman Islands-flagged chemical/oil tanker, sent out a mayday call after being attacked by drones and an anti-ship missile launched by Houthi rebels while transiting the Red Sea on December 18th. The US Navy destroyer USS Carney responded to assist the vessel and assess damages.

questions.question: International Gender Equality Prize is given by which country?
questions.explanation: The International Gender Equality Prize in 2023 was awarded to the Afghan Women Skills Development Center. Prime Minister Petteri Orpo of Finland presented the €300,000 award to Mahbouba Seraj, the Center’s Executive Director, in Tampere on December 11, 2023. The Center is recognized for its crucial work in defending women’s rights and promoting gender equality in Afghanistan, particularly under challenging conditions following the Taliban’s rise to power. International Gender Equality Prize is a government prize administered by the Ministry of Social Affairs and Health, Finland. Tampere has been a partner of the Prize and the host city for the award ceremony since 2017.

questions.question: Recently which IIT has developed BRAHMA-2D model to understand complex flow patterns of large braided rivers like Brahmaputra?
questions.explanation: IIT Guwahati researchers have developed an indigenous river model named BRAHMA-2D (Braided River Aid: Hydro-Morphological Analyzer) to understand complex flow patterns of large braided rivers like Brahmaputra. It combines advanced mathematical modeling with extensive field research to map 3D velocity changes from surface to riverbed. This model can provide valuable insights for design of sustainable structures like embankments, bridges etc along Brahmaputra river. It was successfully validated near Majuli island to demonstrate its practical applicability.

questions.question: Which State Pollution Control Board has launched mobile app ‘Janavani Kalushya Nivarani’?
questions.explanation: The “Janavani- Kalushya Nivarani” app is a free Android app from the Telangana Pollution Control Board (TSPCB). It allows users to register complaints about environmental pollution. The app is available for download from the Android Play Store.

questions.question: TEMPO (Tropospheric Emissions: Monitoring of Pollution sensor) satellite has been launched by?
questions.explanation: NASA’s TEMPO satellite, the Tropospheric Emissions: Monitoring of Pollution sensor, is a ground breaking Earth-observation satellite in geostationary orbit designed to provide hourly measurements of pollutants over North America. TEMPO covers a vast area from Canada’s oil sands to the Yucatán Peninsula and spans across the Atlantic to the Pacific Ocean, enhancing its ability to monitor air pollution comprehensively.
